name: MIPI DSI Display Driver
description: This driver is for displays that use the MIPI DSI display interface available in the ESP32-P4.

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: The interval to re-draw the screen. Defaults to `60s`, use `never` to only manually update the screen via `component.update`.

config_entries.label: Model
config_entries.description: Chosen from the lists of supported chips and models above, or `CUSTOM` for custom displays.

config_entries.label: Enable Pin
config_entries.description: An optional pin to enable the display, if required. A list of pins can be provided for displays that require multiple enable pins. A full pin configuration may be provided to set the pin mode and inverted property. By default the pin will be driven high to enable the display.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Reset Pin
config_entries.description: The RESET pin, if required.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: Auto Clear Enabled
config_entries.description: If the display should be cleared before each update. Defaults to `true` if a lambda or pages are configured, false otherwise.

config_entries.label: Byte Order
config_entries.description: The byte order of the display buffer. Options are `big_endian` and `little_endian` (default). This affects the byte order for the buffer when using 16 bit color depth. The default is appropriate for the majority of displays.

config_entries.label: Color Depth
config_entries.description: The color depth of the display buffer, expressed in bits. Options are `16` (default) and `24`. Preferably should be the same as the `pixel_mode` option.

config_entries.label: Color Order
config_entries.description: Should be one of `bgr` (default) or `rgb`. This specifies the order of the color channels in the display panel. The default is `bgr` for most displays, but some displays may require `rgb`. It does not affect the color order of the display buffer, which is always RGB.

config_entries.label: Dimensions
config_entries.description: Dimensions of the screen, specified either as *width* **x** *height* (e.g `320x240`  ) or with separate config keys. If not provided the dimensions will be determined by the model selected. This is required for the `CUSTOM` model, and is optional for other models. The dimensions are specified in pixels, and the width and height must be greater than 0. The following keys are available:

config_entries.label: Height
config_entries.description: Specifies height of display in pixels.

config_entries.label: Width
config_entries.description: Specifies width of display.

config_entries.label: Offset Height
config_entries.description: Specify an offset for the y-direction of the display. Default is 0.

config_entries.label: Offset Width
config_entries.description: Specify an offset for the x-direction of the display, typically used when an LCD is smaller than the maximum supported by the driver chip. Default is 0

config_entries.label: Draw Rounding
config_entries.description: The rounding factor for drawing operations. Defaults to 2. Some chips require a higher value to avoid display artifacts. Must be a power of 2.

config_entries.label: HSYNC Back Porch
config_entries.description: The horizontal back porch length.

config_entries.label: HSYNC Front Porch
config_entries.description: The horizontal front porch length.

config_entries.label: HSYNC Pulse Width
config_entries.description: The horizontal sync pulse width.

config_entries.label: Init Sequence
config_entries.description: Allows custom initialisation sequences to be added. See below for more information.

config_entries.label: Invert Colors
config_entries.description: Specifies whether the display colors should be inverted. Options are `true` or `false`. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Lambda
config_entries.description: The lambda to use for rendering the content on the display. `it` will be an instance of <APIClass text="DisplayBuffer" path="display::DisplayBuffer" />. See [Display Rendering Engine](https://esphome.io/components/display#display-engine) for more information.

config_entries.label: Lane Bit Rate
config_entries.description: The bit rate of the serial data lanes. No default unless a non-custom model is selected.

config_entries.label: Lanes
config_entries.description: Number of serial data lanes to use - 1 or 2. Default is 2.

config_entries.label: Pages
config_entries.description: Show pages instead of a single lambda. See [Display Pages](https://esphome.io/components/display#display-pages).

config_entries.label: PCLK Frequency
config_entries.description: Set the pixel clock speed. Default is 40MHz.

config_entries.label: Pixel Mode
config_entries.description: Select the interface mode for the display driver. Options are `16bit` (default) and `24bit`.

config_entries.label: Rotation
config_entries.description: Rotate the display presentation in software. Choose one of `0°`, `90°`, `180°`, or `270°`. If the driver chip supports hardware rotation for the given orientation this will be translated to the appropriate hardware command. If hardware rotation is not supported, the display will be rotated in software.

config_entries.label: Transform
config_entries.description: If `rotation` is not sufficient, use this to transform the display. If this option is specified, then the `dimensions` option must also be provided. The value can either be the string `disabled` to disable hardware transform, or a dictionary. For the `CUSTOM` model, use `transform: disabled` if the display does not support it, which will prevent a `rotation` being translated to a hardware transform, otherwise the options below. Note that DSI displays do not support axis swapping so only mirroring of x and y axes is possible, and rotations by 90 or 270 must be handled by software.

config_entries.label: Use Axis Flips
config_entries.description: If true, the driver will use alternate bits in the MADCTL register to implement x and y mirroring. Defaults to false.

config_entries.label: VSYNC Back Porch
config_entries.description: The vertical back porch length.

config_entries.label: VSYNC Front Porch
config_entries.description: The vertical front porch length.

config_entries.label: VSYNC Pulse Width
config_entries.description: The vertical sync pulse width.
